answer: Convert the Go key to a C string with C.CString(key), call C.get_config, and check if the result is nil. If nil, return an empty string and an error. Otherwise, copy the C string to Go with C.GoString(result), free the C memory with C.free_config_string(result), and return the Go string with nil error. Use sync.WaitGroup to coordinate two goroutines calling GetConfig with different keys, printing each result or error to stdout.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  A[Go GetConfig(key)] --> B[C.CString(key)]
  B --> C[C get_config call]
  C --> D{result == NULL?}
  D -->|Yes| E[Return empty string + error]
  D -->|No| F[C.GoString copies to Go]
  F --> G[C.free_config_string frees C memory]
  G --> H[Return Go string + nil error]
